sections.paragraph:
i.q. זָעַק (which see; compare צָחַק and שָׂחַק ) to cry out, especially for aid, Deuteron. 22:24 , 27 Deu 22:27followed by אֶל of pers. Gen 41:55 Jud. 4:3 followed by לְ 2Ch 13:14 also an acc. of the thing, Job 19:7, אֶצְעַק חָמָס “I cry out of wrong,” sc. as done to me.

Piel, to cry out, 2Ki 2:12.

Hiphil, to call together, like הִזְעִיק 1Sa 10:17.

Niphal, pass. of Hiph. to be called together, to run together, Jud. 7:23 , 24 Jdg 7:24 10:17 . Hence-
